definition: The name of the indigenous people or traditional community from which this label or knowledge originates.
notes: Use the official self-designation of the community (endonym) when available. E.g. 'Huni Kuĩ (Kaxinawá)' not just 'Kaxinawá'.

definition: Geographic region or territory associated with this label or traditional knowledge.
notes: Can be a toponym, biome, or Indigenous Territory name. E.g. 'Alto Juruá, Acre'.

definition: Access restriction for this label or knowledge. Controlled values: public / restricted / community-only / sacred.
notes: Implement enforcement at the application layer. SKOS-XL per-label granularity enables different access levels for the same concept's labels.

definition: The indigenous organization, council, or representative body that validated this label.
notes: Required for labels designated as authoritative by the community. E.g. 'Conselho Aty Guasu', 'ASKARJ'.

definition: URI to an audio recording of the correct pronunciation of this label by a community speaker.
notes: Formats: mp3, ogg, wav. Recorded with explicit community consent.

definition: Notes on dialectal variation of this label within the community or language family.
notes: Use skosxl:labelRelation to link dialectal variants as related Label resources.

definition: Vitality status of the language of this label, using UNESCO Ad Hoc Expert Group criteria.
notes: Values: safe / vulnerable / definitely-endangered / severely-endangered / critically-endangered / extinct.

definition: Type of prior informed consent associated with disclosure of this knowledge.
notes: Values: none-required / fpic (Free Prior Informed Consent under Nagoya) / restricted.

definition: Compliance status with the Nagoya Protocol on Access and Benefit-Sharing (CBD, 2010).
notes: Values: compliant / pending / restricted / not-applicable. See: https://www.cbd.int/abs/

definition: Local Contexts Traditional Knowledge (TK) Label applied to this knowledge or label resource.
notes: TK Labels are indigenous-controlled licenses. See: https://localcontexts.org/labels/traditional-knowledge-labels/

definition: Links a label or piece of knowledge to the entity (people, community, or individual) responsible for it.
notes: Core PROV-O property. Required for CARE Collective principle compliance. Domain: any resource.

definition: The primary community consultation, field recording, or ethnobotanical study from which this label was obtained.
notes: More specific than dct:source; use when source was a direct community interaction.

definition: A related resource from which this label is derived (published study, consultation report, herbarium record).
notes: Use a URI where possible (DOI, JBRJ Flora record, etc.).

definition: The community or organization holding intellectual rights over this knowledge.
notes: Follows CARE Authority principle. Applied to ConceptScheme or individual Concept/Label.

definition: License governing use of this knowledge. TK Labels recommended for indigenous knowledge.
Language of the label, preferably using ISO 639-3 codes for indigenous languages.
notes: ISO 639-3 covers most indigenous languages. E.g. 'hux' = Huni Kuĩ; 'tup' = Tupí; 'gnm' = Guarani Mbya. See: https://iso639-3.sil.org/

definition: Links this concept to its Darwin Core vernacular name string.
notes: Bridge property between SKOS-XL Label and Darwin Core. The skosxl:Label with indigenous literalForm IS the vernacularName entity.
